steps.title: Character Concept
steps.description: What kind of hero do you want to play? Pick an archetype and jot a few notes—mechanical choices come next.

steps.title: Choose a Class
steps.description: Your class defines combat role, Max Wounds, and core features.

steps.title: Choose a Subclass
steps.description: At 2nd level in YMIAT you take a subclass—choose yours when building a level 2+ character.

steps.title: Determine Ability Scores
steps.description: Assign Fitness, Insight, and Willpower modifiers using your group's preferred method.

steps.title: Choose a Lineage
steps.description: Lineage traits shape ancestry and fundamental abilities.

steps.title: Choose a Heritage
steps.description: Heritage adds cultural flavor and extra traits. Any heritage works with any lineage—recommendations highlight common pairings.

steps.title: Choose a Background
steps.description: Background reflects life before adventuring—skills, gear, and a defining talent.

steps.title: Starting Equipment
steps.description: Take standard packages or roll starting wealth—confirm with your GM.

steps.title: Review Character
steps.description: Check your choices, copy a summary, or start over.

conceptArchetypes.label: Melee Combatant
conceptArchetypes.hint: Front-line fighter with sword, axe, or fist.

conceptArchetypes.label: Magic User
conceptArchetypes.hint: Spells to harm, heal, or control the field.

conceptArchetypes.label: Ranged Fighter
conceptArchetypes.hint: Bow, crossbow, or thrown weapons at distance.

conceptArchetypes.label: Support Character
conceptArchetypes.hint: Buff allies, heal, and control enemies.

conceptArchetypes.label: Hybrid Character
conceptArchetypes.hint: Blend martial prowess with magic or tactics.

abilityMethods.label: Standard Array
abilityMethods.description: Assign +2, +1, and +0 to Fitness, Insight, and Willpower in any order.

abilityMethods.label: Point-Buy
abilityMethods.description: Start at 0 with 3 points. Each ability may be −1 to +2; lowering one below 0 frees a point.

abilityMethods.label: Rolling (GM)
abilityMethods.description: Rolling is still being adapted for YMIAT—use another method or agree on rolls with your GM.

equipmentMethods.label: Class & background equipment
equipmentMethods.description: Take the gear listed for your class and background—fastest option at the table.

equipmentMethods.label: Starting wealth
equipmentMethods.description: Receive starting gold from your class and buy gear from the equipment lists.

Age. Adulthood within 5 years; lifespan up to a century (some only 20–30 years).

Size. Medium or Small (under 4 feet tall → Small).

Speed. 30 feet.

Animal Instinct. Proficiency in Perception or Survival.

Natural Weapons. Unarmed strikes deal 1 Wound + Fitness modifier (work with your GM on damage type).

Natural Adaptation. Choose one: Avian (fly speed = walk), Agile (climb speed = walk; advantage vs. prone), Aquatic (swim speed = walk; hold breath 20 min), or Sturdy (AC 13 + Fitness without armor; count as one size larger for carry capacity).

PG2 expansion: Fossorial (burrow 20 ft through soft earth) or Quillback (reaction quill strike when hit in melee).

backgrounds.name: Acolyte
backgrounds.teaser: You have spent your life in service to a temple dedicated to a specific god or pantheon, acting as an intermediary between the divine and the mortal world—performing sacred rites, offering blessings, and conducting…
backgrounds.body:
You have spent your life in service to a temple dedicated to a specific god or pantheon, acting as an intermediary between the divine and the mortal world—performing sacred rites, offering blessings, and conducting worshipers into the presence of the divine. You aren't necessarily a cleric; performing sacred rites isn't the same as channeling divine power.

Those who share your faith support you (but only you) at a modest lifestyle, and you can expect free healing and care at any temple, shrine, or established presence of your faith, though you must still provide any material components needed for spells.

Skill Proficiencies: Insight and Religion.

Additional Proficiencies: Calligrapher's supplies, and two languages of your choice.

Equipment: A holy symbol, a prayer book or prayer wheel, 5 sticks of incense, vestments, common clothes, and a pouch containing 15 gp.

Talent: Choose one: Field Medic, Mental Fortitude, or Ritualist.

Equipment: Navigator's tools, magnifying glass, merchant's scale, common and fine clothes, hooded cloak, lantern, empty pouches, and 10 gp.

Talent: Choose one: Covert, Escamotage, or Slippery.

backgrounds.body:
Rigorous training and discipline defined your time in military service.

Skill Proficiencies: Choose two from Animal Handling, Athletics, Medicine, or Survival.

Additional Proficiencies: One tool and one vehicle.

Equipment: Symbol of rank, mess kit, playing cards or dice, common clothes, and 10 gp.

Talent: Choose one: Combat Casting, Combat Conditioning, or Field Medic.
